title: Your Gut Bacteria Run a Body Clock. When It Breaks, Your Sleep Goes With It.
description: A wave of molecular research — including a landmark 2024 Cell Metabolism study — has revealed that gut bacteria don't just influence digestion: they actively regulate the stress-hormone rhythms and clock-gene expression that govern when you sleep and wake. The implications reach from chronic insomnia to Alzheimer's disease. The probiotic industry will try to sell you the answer before the science has found it.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Your Gut Bacteria Run a Body Clock. When It Breaks, Your Sleep Goes With It.
